---
title: "From Heterogeneous Biomedical Data to Patient Digital Twins. "
subtitle: "The Role of Knowledge Graphs in Cyber-Physical and Cyber-Biological Systems for E-Health"
author: "Pasquale De Meo"
institute: "University of Messina"
date: "July 17, 2026"
pdf-engine: xelatex
format:
  beamer:
    theme: "Madrid"
    colortheme: "default"
    fonttheme: "default"
    slidelevel: 2
    aspectratio: 169
    fontsize: 10pt
    incremental: false
    navigation: horizontal
    header-includes:
      - \usepackage{tikz}
      - \usetikzlibrary{positioning,arrows.meta,shapes.geometric,calc,fit}
      - \usepackage{pgfplots}
      - \usepackage{booktabs}
      - \usepackage{graphicx}
      - \usepackage{hyperref}
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \setbeamertemplate{navigation symbols}{}
        \setbeamertemplate{footline}{
          \leavevmode\hbox{
            \begin{beamercolorbox}[wd=.33\paperwidth,ht=2.5ex,dp=1ex,center]{author in head/foot}
              \usebeamerfont{author in head/foot}\insertshortauthor
            \end{beamercolorbox}
            \begin{beamercolorbox}[wd=.34\paperwidth,ht=2.5ex,dp=1ex,center]{title in head/foot}
              \usebeamerfont{title in head/foot}\insertshorttitle
            \end{beamercolorbox}
            \begin{beamercolorbox}[wd=.33\paperwidth,ht=2.5ex,dp=1ex,right]{date in head/foot}
              \usebeamerfont{date in head/foot}\insertshortdate{}\hspace*{2em}
              \insertframenumber{} / \inserttotalframenumber\hspace*{2ex}
            \end{beamercolorbox}
          }
          \vskip0pt
        }
execute:
  echo: false
---


# The Semantic Bridge Thesis {.center}

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}[
  >=Stealth,
  box/.style={draw, rounded corners, align=center, fill=blue!6, inner sep=3pt, font=\footnotesize, text width=3cm, minimum height=9mm}
]
\node[box] (cps) at (0,0)   {CPS / IoT\\sensors \& devices};
\node[box] (kg)  at (5,0)   {Knowledge Graph\\FHIR $\cdot$ SNOMED CT $\cdot$ SOSA};
\node[box] (pdt) at (10,0)  {Patient\\Digital Twin};
\draw[->,very thick,double,double distance=1.2pt] (cps) -- node[fill=white,inner sep=1pt,font=\scriptsize]{semantic bridge} (kg);
\draw[->,very thick,double,double distance=1.2pt] (kg) -- node[fill=white,inner sep=1pt,font=\scriptsize]{reasoning} (pdt);
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
```

- CPS/IoT streams are **syntactically rich but semantically poor**; KGs supply the missing meaning layer.
- A KG makes observations **queryable, reason-able, and clinically comparable**.
- **Lecture thesis:** the *quality of the bridge* (ontologies + pipelines + reasoning), not the sensors, determines clinical trustworthiness.

---

# Roadmap {.center}

| Section | Focus |
|---|---|
| **1. Foundations & Standards** | Ontology stack, architectures |
| **2. CPS-to-KG Pipelines** | Sensor $\rightarrow$ triple architectures |
| **3. Digital Twin & AI Reasoning** | Rule-based / neuro-symbolic, validation |
| **4. Open Challenges** | Scalability, privacy, benchmarks |

We focus on **eight** recent papers (2023--2026).




# 1 · Foundations & Standards
##  *"What language do the sensors speak?"*

- Goal: establish the **ontology vocabulary** and the **conceptual architecture** before any pipeline.
- Anchored by:
  - **Hendawi & Li (2024)** -- clinical terminologies;
  - **Chatterjee et al. (2024)** -- sensor ontologies;
  - **Carbonaro et al. (2023)** -- blueprint.


---

<!--
## Healthcare Ontology Foundations: From Codes to Graphs

- **Core contribution:** A *Comprehensive Personal Health KG (PHKG)* unifying EHR, wearables, insurance, and **social determinants of health (SDoH)** under one OWL schema with **SNOMED CT + ICD-10** mapped to nodes.
- Privacy is **schema-native** (role-based access control embedded in the KG), not bolted on.
- SPARQL exposes **longitudinal/temporal** queries.
- **Ontology used:** OWL · SNOMED CT (clinical) · ICD-10 (administrative).


**Reference.** Hendawi, R., & Li, J. (2024). *Comprehensive personal health knowledge graph for effective management and utilization of personal health data*. In **Proceedings of IEEE AIMHC** (pp. 1–8). IEEE. https://doi.org/10.1109/aimhc59811.2024.00026

---

## Sensor Semantics: The SSN/SOSA Bridge

- **Core contribution:** Annotates **MOX2-5 accelerometer** observations as OWL/RDF triples via W3C **SSN/SOSA**
- Converts **CSV → RDF** and compares real vs. synthetic activity data through SPARQL.
- Formally models **sensor · observation · feature-of-interest · result**, yielding device-agnostic, interoperable activity graphs with an explicit **FHIR integration pathway**.
- **Ontology used:** W3C SSN/SOSA (observation structure), candidate FHIR alignment.

**Reference.** Chatterjee, A., Gerdes, M., Prinz, A., Riegler, M., & Martínez, S. (2024). *Semantic representation and comparative analysis of physical activity sensor observations using MOX2-5 sensor in real and synthetic datasets: A proof-of-concept study*. **Scientific Reports**, *14*, Article 55183. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41598-024-55183-6

---

## Conceptual Blueprint: CONNECTED (PKG + DT)

- **Core contribution:** A systematic review distilled into a **layered architecture** — CPS/IoT acquisition → OWL/FHIR semantic alignment → **Personal Knowledge Graph** (evolving) → **DT simulation**.
- Formalises four requirements rarely co-located: 
  - **semantic interoperability**, 
  - **privacy-by-design**, 
  - **real-time data fusion**, 
  - **temporal reasoning**.
- **Ontology used:** OWL · FHIR (alignment layer); DT engine sits *above* the PKG.


---
-->
## Ontology Stacking: One Patient, Many Vocabularies {.smaller}

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}[
  >=Stealth,
  box/.style={draw, rounded corners, align=center, fill=blue!6, inner sep=3pt, font=\footnotesize, text width=3cm, minimum height=7mm}
]
\node[box] (obs)    at (0,1.8)  {SSN / SOSA\\observation structure};
\node[box] (fhir)   at (0,0.6)  {HL7 FHIR\\exchange \& API};
\node[box] (snomed) at (0,-0.6) {SNOMED CT\\clinical terms};
\node[box] (icd)    at (0,-1.8) {ICD-10\\classification / billing};
\node[box] (owl)    at (5,0)    {OWL 2.0\\logic \& constraints};
\node[box] (phkg)   at (9.2,0)  {Personal Health\\Knowledge Graph};
\draw[->,semithick] (obs) -- (owl);
\draw[->,semithick] (fhir) -- (owl);
\draw[->,semithick] (snomed) -- (owl);
\draw[->,semithick] (icd) -- (owl);
\draw[->,semithick] (owl) -- (phkg);
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
```

- No single standard is canonical; the **KG is where they are reconciled**.
- **Alignment is the failure-prone bottleneck** -- errors propagate to all downstream inferences.




# 2 · CPS-to-KG Integration Pipelines

<!--
## *"From a voltage on a wrist to a triple in a graph"*

- The bridge is an **engineered artifact**: ingestion $\rightarrow$ mapping $\rightarrow$ triple materialisation $\rightarrow$ query.
- Four architectures along one spectrum: **edge vs.\ central, real-time vs.\ batch**.

---

## The Generic Sensor-to-Triple Pipeline

- Each paper is an **instantiation** of stages B$\rightarrow$D.
- They differ in *mapping language*, *where materialisation occurs*, and *update frequency*.
- The key engineering question: **real-time materialisation vs.\ batch re-build?**
-->

## Pourvahab et al. (2025): RDF + R2RML

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}[
  >=Stealth,
  box/.style={draw, rounded corners, align=center, fill=blue!6, inner sep=3pt, font=\footnotesize, text width=2.6cm, minimum height=8mm}
]
\node[box] (w)    at (0,1.2)   {Wearables \& smart devices};
\node[box] (e)    at (0,-0.6)  {EHR / relational DBs};
\node[box] (r2)   at (3.4,0.3) {R2RML mapping rules\\RDB $\rightarrow$ RDF};
\node[box] (dist) at (6.4,2.1) {Distributed graph-build layer\\real-time ingestion};
\node[box] (kg)   at (6.4,0.3) {RDF Knowledge Graph\\OWL-aligned $\cdot$ FHIR vocab};
\node[box] (ml)   at (9.6,0.3) {Graph-based ML pipeline\\inference / extraction};
\node[box] (ci)   at (9.6,-1.6){Clinical intelligence};
\draw[->,semithick] (w) -- (r2);
\draw[->,semithick] (e) -- (r2);
\draw[->,semithick] (r2) -- (kg);
\draw[->,semithick,dashed] (dist) -- node[right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt,font=\scriptsize]{scales} (kg);
\draw[->,semithick] (kg) -- (ml);
\draw[->,semithick] (ml) -- (ci);
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
```

- **R2RML** declaratively maps relational data $\rightarrow$ OWL-aligned triples; a **distributed layer** absorbs real-time IoT volume.
- **Trade-off:** ingestion is real-time, but **graph-ML inference is batch** -- low-latency alerts need a streaming bypass.


---

## Chatterjee et al. (2024): CSV $\rightarrow$ RDF via SSN/SOSA

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}[
  >=Stealth,
  box/.style={draw, rounded corners, align=center, fill=blue!6, inner sep=2pt, font=\scriptsize, text width=2.3cm, minimum height=8mm}
]
\node[box] (s)    at (0,0.6)    {MOX2-5 accelerometer\\physical activity};
\node[box] (csv)  at (2.7,0.6)  {CSV sensor export};
\node[box] (ann)  at (5.4,0.6)  {SSN/SOSA annotation\\sensor / obs / feature / result};
\node[box] (rdf)  at (8.1,0.6)  {OWL-compliant RDF graph};
\node[box] (q)    at (10.8,0.6) {SPARQL query \& comparison};
\node[box] (fhir) at (8.1,-1.2) {FHIR-compliant clinical repo};
\draw[->,semithick] (s) -- (csv);
\draw[->,semithick] (csv) -- (ann);
\draw[->,semithick] (ann) -- (rdf);
\draw[->,semithick] (rdf) -- (q);
\draw[->,semithick] (rdf) -- (fhir);
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
```

- The most concrete **"how to triplify a wearable"** reference in the corpus.
- Pipeline: raw accelerometry $\rightarrow$ activity classification $\rightarrow$ `sosa:Observation` $\rightarrow$ linked to patient.
- **Trade-off:** offline/batch (file export) -- simple and reproducible, but unsuitable for acute monitoring.


---

## Gyrard et al. (2024): A Three-Layer Pipeline

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}[
  >=Stealth,
  box/.style={draw, rounded corners, align=center, fill=blue!6, inner sep=3pt, font=\footnotesize, text width=3.2cm, minimum height=8mm}
]
\node[box] (l1)  at (0,2.2)  {Layer 1 --- Wearable IoT\\EEG $\cdot$ GSR $\cdot$ heart rate};
\node[box] (l2)  at (0,0.7)  {Layer 2 --- Semantic interop\\IoT-Lite + SOSA/SSN $\rightarrow$ RDF};
\node[box] (l3)  at (0,-0.8) {Layer 3 --- KG-driven Digital Twin\\mental-health trajectories};
\node[box] (int) at (5,-0.8) {Personalised interventions};
\node[box] (std) at (5,1.4)  {HL7 FHIR $\cdot$ SNOMED CT $\cdot$ W3C stack};
\draw[->,semithick] (l1) -- (l2);
\draw[->,semithick] (l2) -- (l3);
\draw[->,semithick] (l3) -- (int);
\draw[->,semithick,dashed] (std) -- node[fill=white,inner sep=1pt,font=\scriptsize]{cross-domain reasoning} (l3);
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
```

- Sensor layer $\rightarrow$ semantic annotation (IoT-Lite + SOSA/SSN) $\rightarrow$ KG-driven DT for **mental-health trajectories**.
- **Trade-off:** near-real-time emotion monitoring, but **reasoning is query-driven (semi-batch)** -- good for screening, not crisis intervention.


---



## Real-Time vs.\ Batch: The Universal Pattern

| Architecture | Ingestion | Reasoning |
|---|---|---|
| Pourvahab 2025 | real-time | batch ML |
| Chatterjee 2024 | offline/batch | query-time |
| Gyrard 2024 | near-real-time | semi-batch SPARQL |

- **Universal pattern:** ingestion moves toward real-time; *reasoning stays batch*.
- The gap between them **is** the clinical-latency bottleneck.
- None provides **streaming incremental reasoning** with bounded guarantees.



# 3 · Digital Twin & AI Reasoning
## *"A graph that thinks -- but how, and is it right?"*

- Reasoning families: **rule-based** (OWL-DL / SWRL), **neuro-symbolic / RAG**, **query-driven** temporal inference.
- For each: mechanism $\rightarrow$ validation $\rightarrow$ **clinical applicability gap**.


---

## The Reasoning Landscape

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}[
  >=Stealth,
  box/.style={draw, rounded corners, align=center, fill=blue!6, inner sep=3pt, font=\footnotesize, text width=3cm, minimum height=8mm}
]
\node[box] (r)   at (0,1.2)   {Rule-based\\OWL-DL $\cdot$ SWRL};
\node[box] (n)   at (0,0)     {Neuro-symbolic\\KG embeddings + rules};
\node[box] (q)   at (0,-1.2)  {RAG / query-driven\\LLM over SPARQL/KG};
\node[box] (t)   at (4.7,0)   {Patient Digital Twin};
\node[box] (dss) at (8.7,0)   {Clinical decision support};
\draw[->,semithick] (r) -- (t);
\draw[->,semithick] (n) -- (t);
\draw[->,semithick] (q) -- (t);
\draw[->,semithick] (t) -- (dss);
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
```

- **Rule-based:** transparent, auditable, but brittle -- used by Marfoglia, Kramer, Sarani Rad.
- **Neuro-symbolic / RAG:** promising but *not yet grounded* in deployed clinical systems.
- **Common gap:** scarce prospective clinical validation.


---

## Marfoglia (2026): OWL-DL + MIMO

- **Mechanism:** OWL-DL inference enforces semantic consistency; auto-triggers **gap detection** when sensor coverage is insufficient. MIMO standardises AI model I/O for dynamic composition.
- **Validation:** benchmarked on **stroke-risk prediction** with real-world time-aware streams.
- **Gap:** no prospective trial; OWL-DL latency under concurrent load unquantified.

---

## Kramer (2025): Federated SWRL

- **Mechanism:** Patient Medical Digital Twin in OWL 2.0; **SWRL rules at the edge**; only **OWL class assertions** (never raw data) propagate centrally.
- **Validation:** synthetic scenarios in **diabetes & heart failure**.
- **Gap:** synthetic-only; SWRL coverage depends on expert rule authoring; federation untested at scale.

::: {.callout-note title="Privacy insight"}
Instead of sending *"glucose = 72, patient Rossi, 14:32"*, the edge sends *"this observation is an instance of HypoglycaemiaRisk"*. Reasoning operates on **concepts**, not sensitive values.
:::

---

## Sarani Rad (2024): PHKG-Driven Diabetes Twin

- **Mechanism:** a **Personal Health KG** auto-updates as CGM/EHR/PROM observations arrive; OWL reasoning infers **hypoglycaemia-risk patterns**.
- **Validation:** **real patient cohort**; superior glycaemic-outcome prediction vs.\ rule-based baselines.
- **Gap:** single disease, retrospective cohort, no prospective RCT.

---

## Gyrard (2024): Mental-Health Cross-Check

- **Mechanism:** **temporal SPARQL** queries detect burnout/depression trajectories.
- **Validation:** proof-of-concept linked to SDG-3 -- *no clinical-cohort benchmark*.
- **Gap:** affect lacks clean SNOMED CT representation -- reasoning rests on **under-specified clinical semantics**.

::: {.callout-warning title="Epistemic caveat"}
If the concept you want to infer lacks a solid standard encoding, the inference rests on fragile foundations. **The ontology limits what can be reasoned.**
:::



# 4 · Open Challenges & Research Frontiers
## *"Where the graph still breaks"*

- Synthesise across **all eight papers** on four axes.
- Goal: convert gaps into **three PhD-defining research questions**.


---

## Coverage at a Glance {.smaller}

| Axis | Papers addressing it | Status |
|---|---|---|
| **Scalability** | Pourvahab, Marfoglia | partial -- no incremental reasoning |
| **Temporal reasoning** | Gyrard, Marfoglia, Kramer, Sarani Rad, Carbonaro, Hendawi | mostly query-time, not first-class |
| **Privacy / federation** | Kramer, Hendawi, Carbonaro | no differential privacy or verifiable federation |
| **Evaluation benchmarks** | each paper uses its own | **no shared CPS-KG-PDT benchmark** |

- **Privacy** is the rarest axis; **benchmarks** are inconsistent.

---

## Scalability $\times$ Temporal Reasoning

- CPS streams demand *fresh* graphs; OWL-DL/SWRL reasoning is **non-incremental** -- re-materialisation cost grows with graph size.
- Temporal support is mostly **query-time** (SPARQL) or **scenario-based**, not a first-class model (e.g., valid-time RDF).

---

## Privacy-Preserving & Federated KGs

- **Best:** Kramer propagates *only class assertions*; Hendawi embeds **RBAC in the schema**; Carbonaro elevates **privacy-by-design**.
- **Collective gap:** no differential privacy, no secure multi-party computation, no verifiable federation.
- **Inference-based re-identification** of rare patients is unanalysed.

---

## Evaluation Benchmarks: The Missing Yardstick

- Each paper invents **its own** dataset/metric.
- **No shared benchmark** measuring semantic interoperability *and* inference latency *and* twin fidelity together.
- Synthetic-only validation cannot certify clinical soundness.


---

## Three Open PhD Research Questions

::: {.callout-tip title="RQ1 -- Streaming Temporal Reasoning"}
How to perform **incremental temporal reasoning** over CPS/IoT streams with **bounded, clinically-tolerable latency**?
:::

::: {.callout-tip title="RQ2 -- Verifiably-Private Federated Reasoning"}
Can we design **federated neuro-symbolic reasoning** with **provable privacy guarantees** and full auditability (GDPR Art.\ 22)?
:::

::: {.callout-tip title="RQ3 -- A Shared CPS-KG-PDT Benchmark"}
What benchmark suite would let heterogeneous KG-PDT architectures be compared on equal terms?
:::


---

## Key Takeaways {.center}

- **KGs are the bridge** -- but quality is set by *ontology alignment*, not sensor count.
- **Ingestion is real-time; reasoning is not** -- that gap *is* the clinical-latency problem.
- **Rule-based reasoning dominates**; neuro-symbolic/RAG remain ungrounded clinically.
- **Privacy and benchmarks are the twin blind spots** of the entire corpus.
- A defensible PhD lives where the matrix has **a "No" on a deployment-critical axis**.


---

## References {.smaller}

- Carbonaro et al. (2023). CONNECTED: DTs and PKGs in healthcare. *Front.\ Digital Health*, *5*, 1322428.
- Chatterjee et al. (2024). Semantic representation of MOX2-5 sensor observations. *Sci.\ Reports*, *14*, 55183.
- Gyrard et al. (2024). IoT-based preventive mental health using KGs. *arXiv:2406.13791*.
- Hendawi & Li (2024). Comprehensive PHKG for personal health data. *IEEE AIMHC*, 1--8.
- Kramer (2025). Semantic framework for patient DTs in chronic care. *arXiv:2510.09134*.
- Marfoglia et al. (2026). KG-driven framework for AI-powered patient DTs. *FGCS*, 108380.
- Pourvahab et al. (2025). Unified semantic framework for IoT-healthcare. *ACM SAC*, 3757054.
- Sarani Rad et al. (2024). Personalized diabetes management with DTs. *J.\ Pers.\ Med.*, *14*(4), 359.
